SP26 Data Analysis

Kaylee Fernandez

2026-05-11

Method

Participants

19 participants completed the experiment, comprising 10 L1 Spanish speakers (IDs beginning with 1) and 9 L2 Spanish learners (IDs beginning with 2).

Procedure

Participants completed a three-phase Spanish stress-suffix association task in PsychoPy. The pre-test consisted of 360 trials across 6 blocks, followed by a 120-trial training phase with feedback, and concluded with a 360-trial post-test (parallel structure to the pre-test). On each trial, participants heard a spoken stimulus and selected between PRESENTE and PASADO response options.

Pre- and post-test stimuli were drawn from six conditions, labeled by verb stress (paroxytone, oxytone), noun match (Match, Mismatch), and verb match (Match, Mismatch), with splice variants from two source nouns (Table 1). Training stimuli comprised two conditions: parox (paroxytone present, condition a) and oxy (oxytone past, condition b).

Analysis

Reaction time (RT) was analyzed three ways: (a) on correct trials only, (b) on incorrect trials only, and (c) on all trials regardless of accuracy. All RT measures used the same trimming criteria, excluding responses faster than 200 ms or slower than 5,000 ms. All analyses were run in R using the tidyverse. Because the number of valid trials per cell varied across participants (e.g., for incorrect-trial RTs), group means were computed by first averaging within participant and then across participants, ensuring equal weighting of each participant.

Table 1. Condition coding for pre- and post-test stimuli.
Cond Name Verb stress Verb match Suffix tense Noun match Splice
a parox_nounmatch_verbmatch paroxytone Match present Match a
b oxy_nounmismatch_verbmatch oxytone Match past Mismatch a
c parox_nounmatch_verbmismatch paroxytone Mismatch past Match a
d parox_nounmismatch_verbmatch paroxytone Match present Mismatch c
e oxy_nounmatch_verbmismatch oxytone Mismatch present Match c
f oxy_nounmatch_verbmatch oxytone Match past Match c
Table 2. Condition coding for training stimuli.
Cond Name Verb stress Tense
a parox paroxytone present
b oxy oxytone past

Results

Overall Performance by Phase and Group

Mean accuracy and reaction time (correct, incorrect, all trials) by group and phase.
Group Phase Accuracy RT correct RT incorrect RT all n incorrect n
L1 Pre 97.5% 1894 ms 2060 ms 1898 ms 97 10
L1 Training 77.0% 2032 ms 2356 ms 2076 ms 299 10
L1 Post 93.7% 1784 ms 1925 ms 1791 ms 237 10
L2 Pre 90.6% 2273 ms 2790 ms 2295 ms 303 9
L2 Training 61.4% 2392 ms 2605 ms 2449 ms 417 9
L2 Post 92.4% 1958 ms 2078 ms 1961 ms 245 9

L1 Spanish speakers showed accuracy at pre-test (M = 97.5%, RTcorrect = 1894 ms), training (M = 77.0%, RTcorrect = 2032 ms), post-test (M = 93.7%, RTcorrect = 1784 ms).

L2 Spanish speakers showed accuracy at pre-test (M = 90.6%, RTcorrect = 2273 ms), training (M = 61.4%, RTcorrect = 2392 ms), post-test (M = 92.4%, RTcorrect = 1958 ms).

From pre- to post-test, L1 speakers showed an accuracy change of -3.8 percentage points and a correct-trial RT change of -110 ms.

From pre- to post-test, L2 speakers showed an accuracy change of +1.8 percentage points and a correct-trial RT change of -315 ms.

Mean accuracy (left) and reaction time on correct trials (right) for pre- and post-test phases by group. Error bars represent standard error of the mean. Dashed line indicates chance performance.

Reaction Time: Correct, Incorrect, and All Trials

Mean reaction time at pre- and post-test by group, computed three ways: correct trials only (left), incorrect trials only (centre), and all trials regardless of accuracy (right).

Accuracy (top) and reaction time on correct trials (bottom) at pre- and post-test by condition, separately for each group. See Table 1 for condition definitions. Error bars represent standard error of the mean.

Training Phase

During training, L1 speakers reached an overall accuracy of 77.0% (RTcorrect = 2032 ms, RTincorrect = 2356 ms).

During training, L2 speakers reached an overall accuracy of 61.4% (RTcorrect = 2392 ms, RTincorrect = 2605 ms).

Training accuracy and reaction time (correct, incorrect, all trials) by group and condition.
Group Condition Accuracy RT correct RT incorrect RT all n incorrect
L1 oxy 79.8% 1946 ms 2259 ms 1982 ms 129
L1 parox 74.2% 2137 ms 2403 ms 2170 ms 170
L2 oxy 60.6% 2408 ms 2637 ms 2461 ms 213
L2 parox 62.2% 2389 ms 2586 ms 2440 ms 204

Training accuracy (left) and reaction time on correct trials (right) by condition and group. Error bars represent standard error of the mean. Dashed line indicates chance performance.

Training phase accuracy (left) and reaction time on correct trials (right) across 10-trial bins by group. Error bars represent standard error of the mean.
